description_long: This object consists of three wooden crates stacked together, each featuring a classic slatted design with prominent diagonal cross-bracing on the visible faces. The wood appears weathered and unfinished, giving the crates a rustic, utilitarian look suitable for urban storage or transport. The crates are open at the top, revealing their hollow interiors, and the slats are spaced to allow for ventilation or easy inspection of contents. There are no visible handles, locks, or labeling areas, emphasizing a simple, functional construction. The corners are reinforced with small round holes, possibly for securing or stacking purposes. The overall arrangement suggests these crates are designed to be stackable and robust for use in garages, warehouses, or outdoor urban environments.
description: A set of three weathered wooden crates with open tops and cross-braced sides, stacked together for storage or transport. The crates have a rustic, utilitarian appearance and are suitable for urban or industrial settings.
description_view_0: Three wooden crates stacked with the top crate slightly offset, showing cross-braced sides and open tops.
description_view_1: Front view of the stacked crates, highlighting the slatted tops and the alignment of the cross-bracing.
description_view_2: Angled view showing the open tops and the arrangement of the crates, with clear visibility of the wood grain and slats.
description_view_3: Side view emphasizing the stacked configuration and the uniformity of the cross-braced wooden panels.
